_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Ability to distinguish between problems that require machine learning and problems that can be solved more simply and reliably with rule-based logic or statistical methods.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Ability AI-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-S-001 (AI, cosine: 0.515), LS-S-009 (LS, cosine: 0.499).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.416). Shared vocabulary: [problems]. NICE-only terms [skill, solving] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, based, distinguish, learning, logic, machine] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.884 (P=0.919, R=0.851). P exceeds R by 0.068 — the NICE element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "Skill in solving problems" and "Ability to distinguish between problems that require machine learning and proble" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.578 > STS 0.416 (gap +0.162). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [problems] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.9%. Shared tokens: [problems]. NICE-only: [skill, solving]. WIDAI-only: [ability, based, distinguish, learning, logic, machine].

signals.detail: STS 0.416 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.162 with low STS 0.416. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
